light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

generated_text.quick_read: Trout fishing on the Toccoa River is fair to good with steady flow and moderate conditions. Recent stocking enhances opportunities using eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.
generated_text.best_window: The best fishing window is day 2 morning due to low rain probability, mild sky cover, and moderate humidity, providing stable conditions that favor trout feeding near runs, seams, and structure.
generated_text.current_conditions: USGS reports a flow of 230 cfs, gage height of 1.21 ft, water temperature unavailable, and turbidity data not available.
generated_text.weather_setup: Current weather includes partly cloudy skies averaging 30% cover, pressure at 30.06 inHg stable, light west winds averaging 3.5 mph with gusts up to 13.8 mph, and a moderate rain chance with 0.1 inches expected.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout are likely to position themselves in runs, seams, and near structure, feeding comfortably while staying close to cover under moderate light. Use a balanced nymph approach prioritizing clean drifts with stocked-trout attractors like egg patterns, squirmy worms, and duracell flies; caution notes are not applicable.
